company_type: Quick Service Restaurant (QSR) - Pizza Delivery and Carryout

key_suppliers: Single supplier for U.S. pizza cheese (contract expires December 2029)
Single supplier for majority of U.S. meat toppings (contract expires December 2025)
Coca-Cola (exclusive beverage supplier, contract expires December 31, 2030)
manufacturing_locations: United States (22 regional dough manufacturing and supply chain centers, 2 thin crust facilities, 1 vegetable processing center)
Canada (5 regional dough manufacturing and supply chain centers)
dependencies: Single-source supplier for U.S. pizza cheese
Single-source supplier for majority of U.S. meat toppings
Cheese represents approximately 25% of food basket purchased by Company-owned stores
Reliance on third-party suppliers for ingredients meeting strict quality standards
Dependence on frequent deliveries of fresh food products
risks: Shortages, interruptions or disruptions in supply or delivery of fresh food products and store equipment
Increased demand, capacity constraints, expiration of existing agreements, problems in production or distribution, product recalls, financial or other difficulties of suppliers, inclement weather
Supply chain disruptions from capacity, volume, systems, staffing and operational challenges
Climate change effects on weather impacts and operations
Supplier concentration risk (single suppliers for key ingredients)
Need to replace suppliers if current agreements expire or suppliers fail to meet requirements

regulatory_risks: Federal Trade Commission (FTC) rules and state laws regulating franchise offer and sale
Fair Labor Standards Act and state/local laws on minimum wage, overtime, working conditions, citizenship requirements
Minimum wage increases (including California AB 1228 raising minimum wage for fast food chains effective April 1, 2024)
Joint employer liability under National Labor Relations Act (NLRA) and state wage/hour laws (including California AB-5)
Privacy and data protection laws (California Privacy Rights Act, New York SHIELD Act, state comprehensive data protection laws)
Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) compliance
Health, safety, sanitation, building, zoning, environmental protection regulations
Franchise relationship regulations (terminations, refusal to renew)
Antitrust laws (including claims against franchisors for no-poach clauses)
Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (health insurance requirements)
Tax law changes and increases in enacted tax rates
Laws targeting hidden fees and advertising/marketing price disclosures (California, Minnesota)
Foreign investment regulations in international markets
National and local laws in international markets (similar to U.S. regulations)

sanction_risks: National and international conflicts, sanctions, acts of war or terrorist acts
Increases in anti-American sentiment and identification of Domino's as an American brand (including due to geopolitical tensions in the Middle East)
Russia market: Master franchisee filed for bankruptcy in August 2023; no royalties received since Russian invasion of Ukraine in February 2022
Tariffs announced on February 1, 2025 on imports from Canada and Mexico; potential for retaliatory tariffs or trade policy changes
Expropriation of private enterprises in foreign markets
Foreign policy changes and geopolitical risk
